title: Types of drift and what each means
description: Data drift (input distribution shifts), concept drift (input-output relationship changes), and schema drift (structure changes) need different responses.
body: Use when interpreting a monitoring alert. Covariate/data drift means inputs shifted but the underlying relationship holds; concept drift means the relationship between inputs and target changed (the model is now wrong even on familiar inputs); schema drift means structure changed (new/renamed columns). Each demands a different fix: investigate source vs retrain vs update the contract. Do not lump them together. Actions: classify the drift before responding. Validation signals include a drift metric plus evidence of which type. Risk boundary: misclassifying concept drift as benign covariate drift leaves a quietly-wrong model in production.
